data.title: Cathie Wood Buys Netflix and AI Stocks, Dumps Beam, PINS, and GitLab Shares
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) disclosed their daily ETF trades for Wednesday, January 21, 2026. The firm added to streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) and several AI stocks, while trimming exposure to select biotech and software stocks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Stocks Did Wood Buy on January 21?

1. Netflix Stock – Wood made her largest buy of the day in Netflix, purchasing 83,368 shares through the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) for about $7.27 million, signaling confidence in Netflix's long-term growth. Netflix shares fell 2% yesterday even after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results as investors remain more focused on Netflix's proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) than the quarter itself.  Netflix posted fourth-quarter revenue of $12.1 billion, modestly above Wall Street expectations of $11.97 billion, while adjusted earnings came in at $0.56 per share, also slightly ahead of forecasts of $0.55 per share.

2. Tempus AI Stock (TEM) – Wood bought 89,501 shares through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG), investing about $5.98 million to expand her AI-driven healthcare exposure.  TEM shares dipped 2.2% on January 21. The move comes as AI tools see wider use in health care data work.

3. WeRide Stock (WRD) – Wood bought 111,439 shares of WeRide through the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ), worth about $945,000. The buy follows earlier purchases as self-driving tests and pilot programs continue to expand. WRD shares rose 4.3% on January 21.

4. Trimble (TRMB) – Wood bought 8,817 shares of Trimble through the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX) for about $614,000. The purchase comes as demand remains steady for industrial software and positioning tools used in construction and aerospace projects.

To compare how these newly added positions stack up, let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Archer Aviation (ACHR): What BlackRock's 8.1% Stake Means for Investors
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) has filed an amended Schedule 13G showing it owns about 8.1% of Archer Aviation (ACHR). The filing confirms BlackRock holds nearly 53 million Class A shares, making it one of Archer's largest shareholders.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, the filing was made under Rule 13d-1(b), which applies to passive institutional investors. BlackRock clearly stated the shares were acquired in the ordinary course of business and not for the purpose of influencing control. In the filing, the firm said the shares "were not acquired and are not held for the purpose of or with the effect of changing or influencing the control of the issuer."

As a result, the disclosure signals steady ownership rather than a strategic move. BlackRock holds sole voting and selling power over the shares, which is typical for positions spread across index funds and managed accounts. There is no shared control and no indication of board involvement or pressure on management.

Meanwhile, ACHR shares dropped 2.08% on Wednesday, closing at $8.47.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise on Easing Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday morning, driven by easing geopolitical tensions after President Donald Trump announced at the World Economic Forum that he would not proceed with his planned tariffs on February 1 and mentioned that no force would be used to acquire Greenland. Also, investors await the release of the personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index reading. 

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.82%, 0.52%, and 0.28%, respectively, at 8:22 a.m. EST on January 22.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, the three major averages closed higher. The Dow added 1.21%, while the S&P 500 gained 1.16%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.18%. Trump announced he would halt new tariffs on Europe scheduled for February 1. He also revealed a "framework" agreement on Greenland, easing global investor worries over a forced acquisition.

Turning to trending stocks, Procter & Gamble (PG) shares were down 1.5% after the consumer goods giant missed fiscal second-quarter revenue estimates. Also, McCormick (MKC) stock was down more than 6% after reporting dismal Q4 2025 earnings. Mobileye Global (MBLY) stock was down more than 4% after announcing a disappointing full-year outlook.

data.title: Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Motors as it Takes to the F1 Road in Major Mercedes Sponsorship Deal
data.description:
Shares in tech titan Microsoft (MSFT) motored higher today after it sealed a major multi-year sponsorship deal with the Mercedes Formula One team.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Branding and Technology

Microsoft branding will appear on the sides of the new Mercedes F1 car and front wing. But it will also aim to boost efficiencies and innovations in the team by utilizing its "trusted cloud and enterprise AI technologies across the Mercedes business from the factory to the racetrack."

This could be important given new regulations for 2026, which will see smaller cars and a greater reliance on battery power.

The two parties did not reveal any financial details but it has been reported that the deal could be worth $60 million a year for the F1 team, whose drivers are Englishman George Russell and Italian Kimi Antonelli.

The team won eight constructors' titles in a row between 2014 and 2021, but have been left in the rearview mirror in recent years with the success of Red Bull and McLaren.

Mercedes will hold an online launch event on February 2 with two more tests in Bahrain before the season starts in Australia on March 8.

"Our sport is driven by those who lead through innovation," said Toto Wolff, CEO and Team Principal, Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS F1 Team.

Microsoft commercial business CEO, Judson Althoff, added: "This partnership puts Microsoft's cloud and enterprise AI technologies at the heart of racing performance, where milliseconds matter, and data determines outcomes. We are harnessing data and turning it into real-time intelligence that powers faster decisions, smarter strategies and sustained competitive advantage — both on and off the track."

More U.S. Viewers

Formula One, which has around 800 million fans globally, is proving increasingly attractive to tech titans. Companies like Google (GOOGL) have established similar technology collaborations, such as with reigning world champions McLaren.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: PG Earnings: Procter & Gamble Stock Slips on Mixed Results
data.description:
Procter & Gamble (PG) stock dropped on Thursday alongside the release of the consumer packaged goods company's Fiscal Q2 2026 earnings report. The report included adjusted earnings per share of $1.88, which was just above Wall Street's estimate of $1.86. Its adjusted EPS was also in line with what was reported in Fiscal Q2 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Procter & Gamble reported revenue of $22.2 billion during the quarter, which barely failed to meet analysts estimate of $22.3 billion. It also only represented a 1% increase year-over-year from $21.9 billion. The company also noted that its organic sales for the period were unchanged when compared to the same time last year.
data.author: William White

data.title: Apple Stock (AAPL) Gains on Resistance to Demand for Financials in India Antitrust Probe
data.description:
Apple's (AAPL) shares edged higher early Thursday after the U.S. tech giant put on some resistance to the Indian competiton authority's demand for its global financial records as part of watchdog's probe.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a January 15 filing seen by Reuters, the tech company asked an high court in Delhi to stop the Competition Commission of India from demanding its financial while it challenges the watchdog's new law. The new regulation could see Apple fined up to $38 billion for allegedly abusing its dominant market position on its app store.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/22/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb after Indexes Rebound, Inflation Data in Sight
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose early Thursday after the three indexes marked a major rebound in the previous session, aided by President Trump's easing tariff stance. Trump announced at the World Economic Forum that he would not proceed with his planned tariffs on February 1 and mentioned that no force would be used to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.77%, 0.52%, and 0.29%, respectively, at 3:59 a.m. EST on January 22.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, the three major averages closed higher. The Dow added 1.21%, while the S&P 500 gained 1.16%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.18%. Trump announced he would halt new tariffs on Europe scheduled for February 1. He also revealed a "framework" agreement on Greenland, easing global investor worries over a forced acquisition.

Turning toward the economic calendar, investors now focus on Thursday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge. Data on weekly initial jobless claims will also be released today.

On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Intel (INTC), Visa (V), and GE Aerospace (GE).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.23%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $60.49 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,827 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today following U.S. President Trump's announcement of a "framework" deal for Greenland and his decision to walk back planned tariffs on Europe.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets also rebounded on January 22 amid a cooling geopolitical environment.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.17%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.14%, while the Shenzhen Component added 0.67%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei advanced 1.73%, and the Topix surged 0.74%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth
